title: Data as a measurement process
description: I treat survey responses, social media posts, news articles, and LLM annotations as outputs of measurement systems rather than neutral data points. This connects ASHA response behavior, immigration narratives, AI discourse, and public sentiment.
question: Explain Sagnik's research agenda around treating data as a measurement process. Connect this to Total Survey Error, narrative indicators, ASHA, and LLM annotation.

title: Survey methodology as infrastructure for AI
description: My survey training gives structure to problems that ML systems often ignore: who is represented, who is missing, how labels are generated, how measurement error enters, and how uncertainty should be carried into downstream inference.
question: Explain how Sagnik connects survey methodology with machine learning. Include sampling, response rates, uncertainty, and data-centric ML.

title: Narratives as social indicators
description: Projects such as FrameScope, Immigration Narratives, GTI/media framing, and EV sentiment ask how public narratives can be measured, compared, and interpreted without treating them as direct substitutes for reality.
question: Explain Sagnik's work on narrative and framing as measurable social signals. Include FrameScope, immigration narratives, GTI, and EV sentiment.

title: Research systems, not just static analyses
description: I am increasingly interested in building reusable systems: dashboards, retrieval-augmented assistants, annotation workflows, and reproducible pipelines that make research outputs easier to inspect, update, and communicate.
question: Explain Sagnik's interest in building research systems rather than only static analyses. Include FrameScope, SagnikGPT, dashboards, and reproducible pipelines.
